evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my Kotlin Multiplatform app. Here's my empty shared screen model: EmptyScreenModel.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes. Please show me the complete updated screen model code in your response.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md and returns complete BarcodeBatch integration code inline in the shared commonMain screen model (DataCaptureContext.initialize, Camera + createRecommendedCameraSettings, BarcodeBatch.forContext, BarcodeBatchSettings.barcodeBatchSettings(), a BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay wired via addOverlay, BarcodeBatchListener with onSessionUpdated, lifecycle hooks, and teardown), and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions the com.scandit.datacapture.kmp:core and com.scandit.datacapture.kmp:barcode Gradle dependencies
Setup checklist mentions the Scandit/datacapture-kmp-spm Swift package for iOS
Setup checklist mentions CAMERA permission (Android) and NSCameraUsageDescription (iOS)
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used (not forLicenseKey, which is not the KMP idiom shown in the reference)
BarcodeBatchSettings.barcodeBatchSettings() is used to construct settings (not a direct BarcodeBatchSettings() constructor)
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.QR is enabled
BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is used
Camera.getDefaultCamera( is used and its nullable result is handled (semantic)
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource( is called
BarcodeBatch.forContext( is used (not a direct BarcodeBatch constructor, and not forDataCaptureContext which is the native Android name)
addListener( is called or equivalent listener registration
A BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is created via withBarcodeBatch( or withBarcodeBatchForView(
The overlay is explicitly added to the view with addOverlay( — not assumed to auto-attach (semantic)
BarcodeBatchListener is implemented with onSessionUpdated
onSessionUpdated signature has parameters (barcodeBatch: BarcodeBatch, session: BarcodeBatchSession, frameData: FrameData)
Camera is turned on with switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.ON) in a start/resume lifecycle hook
Camera is turned off with switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF) in a stop/pause lifecycle hook
barcodeBatch.removeListener( is called during teardown
dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeBatch) is called during teardown (not removeCurrentMode())

evals.prompt: I want to highlight EAN-13 barcodes in green and CODE128 barcodes in blue in my MatrixScan Batch screen model. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener, overrides brushForTrackedBarcode to return colored brushes per symbology, and assigns the listener via overlay.setListener(this) — a function call, not a property assignment.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener is implemented
brushForTrackedBarcode is overridden
EAN13_UPCA returns a green Brush
CODE128 returns a blue Brush
The listener is assigned with overlay.setListener(this) as a function call, not overlay.listener = this as a property assignment (semantic)
The answer mentions this customization requires the MatrixScan AR add-on license
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, DataCaptureView, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch screen model I want to log all currently tracked barcodes — their tracking ID and data — on every frame. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill updates onSessionUpdated to iterate over session.trackedBarcodes, logging each TrackedBarcode's identifier and barcode.data.

assertions.text: session.trackedBarcodes is accessed
Each TrackedBarcode's identifier is used
Each TrackedBarcode's barcode.data is used
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the user taps a highlighted barcode in my MatrixScan Batch screen model, I want to notify the UI with that barcode's data. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener, overrides onTrackedBarcodeTapped to read trackedBarcode.barcode.data, and assigns the listener via overlay.setListener(this).

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener is implemented
onTrackedBarcodeTapped is overridden
onTrackedBarcodeTapped signature has parameters (overlay: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, trackedBarcode: TrackedBarcode)
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read inside onTrackedBarcodeTapped
The listener is assigned with overlay.setListener(this) as a function call, not a property assignment (semantic)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to overlay a custom native label showing the barcode's data on top of each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan Batch screen model, for both Android and iOS. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay via withBarcodeBatch and an explicit addOverlay call, implements BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener.viewForTrackedBarcode returning a NativeView built by a platform-supplied factory lambda, assigns the listener via the overlay.listener property, and notes the MatrixScan AR add-on requirement.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.withBarcodeBatch(barcodeBatch) is used
The advanced overlay is explicitly added to the view with addOverlay( (semantic — no ForView factory exists for the advanced overlay)
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener is implemented
viewForTrackedBarcode is overridden and returns a NativeView?
viewForTrackedBarcode signature has parameters (overlay: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, trackedBarcode: TrackedBarcode)
The view is built by a platform-supplied factory (e.g. a lambda) rather than constructed directly in shared code (semantic — commonMain cannot construct android.view.View or UIView directly)
The listener is assigned with advancedOverlay.listener = ... as a property assignment, not a setListener( function call (semantic)
The MatrixScan AR add-on requirement is mentioned
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I already show a custom view via BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay in my MatrixScan Batch screen model, but it sits on top of the barcode. I want it anchored to the top-center of the barcode and offset upward so it floats just above the code. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill overrides anchorForTrackedBarcode to return Anchor.TOP_CENTER and offsetForTrackedBarcode to return a PointWithUnit built from FloatWithUnit values in MeasureUnit.FRACTION (e.g. y = -1f) so the view floats above the barcode.

assertions.text: anchorForTrackedBarcode is overridden and returns Anchor.TOP_CENTER
offsetForTrackedBarcode is overridden and returns a PointWithUnit
The PointWithUnit is constructed from FloatWithUnit values (not DoubleWithUnit, which is the Flutter name) (semantic)
MeasureUnit.FRACTION is used for the offset unit
Anchor is imported from com.kmp.datacapture.core.common.geometry

offsetForTrackedBarcode signature has parameters (overlay: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, trackedBarcode: TrackedBarcode) with no extra view parameter (semantic — the KMP signature has no third view argument)
Existing advanced overlay and integration code is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch screen model I want to remove a barcode from my own on-screen list as soon as it leaves the camera view. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill reads session.removedTrackedBarcodes in onSessionUpdated and removes matching entries from the app's own state keyed by tracking ID, explaining that removedTrackedBarcodes contains only Int identifiers.

assertions.text: session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed in onSessionUpdated
removedTrackedBarcodes is treated as a list of Int tracking identifiers, not TrackedBarcode objects (semantic)
The removed tracking IDs are used to remove entries from the app's own collection
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want my MatrixScan Batch screen model to beep and vibrate each time a new barcode starts being tracked. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill explains BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback and emits it manually: creates a single reusable Feedback via Feedback.defaultFeedback() (KMP has no public Feedback constructor) and calls emit() in onSessionUpdated when session.addedTrackedBarcodes is non-empty.

assertions.text: The answer explains BarcodeBatch does not emit feedback automatically and it must be emitted manually (semantic)
Feedback.defaultFeedback() is used to create the Feedback instance (not a multi-argument Feedback constructor, which does not exist on KMP) (semantic)
Feedback is imported from com.kmp.datacapture.core.feedback
feedback.emit() is called inside onSessionUpdated
emit() is gated on session.addedTrackedBarcodes being non-empty (so it fires only on newly tracked barcodes)
A single Feedback instance is created once and reused, not constructed every frame (semantic)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch factory, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved
